channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: WHO chief says US has outstanding financial obligations despite planned exit
item.description: Ayon sa ulat, sinabi ng pinuno ng World Health Organization (WHO) na may natitira pang obligasyong pinansyal ang Estados Unidos kahit ito ay nasa proseso ng pag-alis o pagbabawas ng ugnayan sa organis...

item.title: Trump maintains blockade on Iran as tensions over Strait of Hormuz escalate and Putin suggests diplomatic path
item.description: US President Donald Trump has refused to lift a naval blockade on Iran’s ports, insisting that sanctions and military pressure will remain in place until Tehran agrees to a deal addressing its nuclear...

item.title: Trucking industry says most carriers won’t change speeds during Alberta’s QEII 120 km/h pilot
item.description: A pilot project raising the speed limit to 120 km/h on Alberta’s Queen Elizabeth II Highway (QEII) is not expected to significantly change how large trucking companies operate, according to the Albert...

item.title: CDC Reports Outbreak of Antibiotic-Resistant Salmonella Linked to Backyard Chickens in 13 States
item.description: The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) has issued a warning about an ongoing outbreak of antibiotic-resistant Salmonella bacteria connected to backyard poultry across the United States. ...

item.title: Tanzania obtains $1.27 billion financing to advance central SGR railway expansion
item.description: Tanzania has secured a major financing package worth $1.277 billion (about Sh3.3 trillion) to continue construction of its Standard Gauge Railway (SGR), specifically covering the Makutupora to Isaka s...

item.title: Lanzerac Wine Estate challenges SARB forfeiture order over R220m property linked to Markus Jooste
item.description: The luxury Lanzerac Wine Estate in Stellenbosch, previously associated with the late controversial businessman Markus Jooste, is taking legal action against the South African Reserve Bank (SARB). The ...

item.title: Australia’s proposed tech levy to fund journalism offers benefits but faces significant political and industry risks
item.description: The Albanese government has proposed a new scheme, the News Bargaining Incentive (NBI), aimed at strengthening Australian journalism by requiring major digital platforms such as Google, Meta and TikTo...

item.title: Three Islamabad High Court judges transferred after JCP approval amid concerns over transparency
item.description: The Ministry of Law and Justice has officially notified the transfer of three judges from the Islamabad High Court (IHC) to other high courts under Article 200 of the Constitution, following approval ...

item.title: Princess Charlotte’s blue nail polish noted in new Wales family anniversary portrait
item.description: A new family portrait released by the Prince and Princess of Wales to mark their 15th wedding anniversary has drawn attention to a small but notable detail: Princess Charlotte’s blue nail polish. The ...

item.title: Zero Trust, FIDO2, and Just-in-Time Access as Foundations for Secure Digital Government in 2026
item.description: The digital transformation of the public sector in 2026 has shifted focus from simply offering online services to prioritizing robust cybersecurity, identity management, and operational resilience. Wi...

item.title: Do School Lockdown Drills Improve Safety or Increase Anxiety Among Students?
item.description: School lockdown drills have become a routine part of student life in the United States, even for children as young as four years old, due to the frequency of school shootings. In 2026 alone, dozens of...

item.title: Four CAFGU members wounded in retaliatory attacks by Dawlah Islamiya remnants in Maguindanao del Sur
item.description: Four members of the Citizens Armed Forces Geographical Unit (CAFGU) were injured in two separate early morning attacks carried out by remnants of the Dawlah Islamiya group in Datu Hoffer, Maguindanao ...

item.title: Suspect in six-year-old boy's kidnapping and murder killed during police operation in Hafizabad
item.description: In a tragic incident from Chak Sabu area of Pindi Bhattian tehsil in Hafizabad district, Punjab, a suspect identified as 40-year-old Zafar Hashmat was killed on Wednesday during a police encounter. Th...

item.title: Jigawa Receives Over N2.57 Billion Ecological Fund in 2026 But Still Warns of Severe Flooding Risks
item.description: Jigawa State don collect more than ₦2.57 billion from the Ecological Fund throughout 2026 to fight flooding, erosion and other environmental problems. The money was released monthly, starting from ₦14...

item.title: Wizz Air CEO warns of potential airline collapses in September due to sustained high jet fuel prices
item.description: The CEO of Wizz Air, József Váradi, has warned that several European airlines risk closure by September if jet fuel prices remain at their current elevated levels. Speaking to The Telegraph, he explai...

item.title: Albanese links social cohesion to tax reforms and national resilience in budget push
item.description: Crikey analyses Prime Minister Anthony Albanese's increasing use of the term 'social cohesion' in his political messaging. According to reports, Albanese is arguing that social cohesion is at stake wh...

item.title: Vine Reboot App Divine, Backed by Jack Dorsey, Now Available on App Stores with Restored Video Archive
item.description: Divine, a revival of the popular short-form video app Vine, has officially launched to the public on the Apple App Store and Google Play. The app restores access to roughly 500,000 original six-second...

item.title: Regional allies of INDIA bloc to meet in Lucknow to form coordinated strategy post assembly poll results
item.description: Key regional parties within the Opposition’s INDIA bloc are planning to hold a meeting in Lucknow after the declaration of assembly election results in several states, including Assam, West Bengal, Ke...

item.title: Biden Administration Mulls Tariffs, Sparking Trade Concerns with Canada
item.description: Concerns are mounting regarding the future of Canada-U.S. trade relations as the Biden administration explores potential tariffs and investigates vulnerabilities in North American supply chains. Sever...

item.title: Body of Teen Who Drowned in River Yala Recovered After Search in Vihiga
item.description: A family in Vihiga County is mourning after the body of a young man, Bramwel Musalia, who drowned in River Yala, was finally retrieved. The recovery brought an end to a tense and emotional search that...
